title: Today's Tech Brief: AI in the Spotlight
summary: A quick look at the latest in AI: Trump's executive order, Microsoft's new AI model, and more.
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for Trump signs executive order to review AI models before they’re released
imageCaption: Trump signs executive order to review AI models before they’re released (theverge.com)

body.text: I've got my coffee in hand and I'm ready to dive into the latest tech headlines. Today, we're talking AI - specifically, a new executive order from Trump and a fresh AI model from Microsoft.

First up, President Trump has signed an executive order that aims to review AI models before they're released to the public. This move is all about ensuring that these powerful models aren't used for nefarious purposes. The order doesn't force AI companies to share their models with the government, but rather encourages them to do so voluntarily. It's an interesting step in the ongoing conversation around AI safety and regulation.

Next, Microsoft has unveiled its first advanced reasoning AI model, called MAI-Thinking-1. This is one of seven new models announced at Build 2026, and it's a big deal for the tech giant. The idea behind this model is to enable more complex problem-solving and decision-making - think of it like a super-smart assistant that can actually reason with you.

It's worth noting that these developments are part of a larger trend: AI is getting smarter, and it's getting more attention from governments and regulators. As these models become more powerful, it's clear that we need to have a conversation about how they're used and how they're regulated.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
